ground_truth.description: Photo of Uranienborg Kirke in Oslo around sunrise. Classical Oslo buildings on either side. Should recognize Scandinavian town, hopefully Oslo. With metadata hopefully specific church.
ground_truth.photographer_description: A photo of Uranienborg kirke in Oslo around sunrise. On either side are classical Oslo buildings. Based on the photo alone, it should recognize a Scandinavian town, hopefully Oslo. With metadata, it can hopefully also get the specific church. Summertime around sunrise, clear skies.

details.description: A straight residential street in Oslo terminates at a prominent red‑brick church standing slightly elevated on a stone base. The church has a tall, slender spire with a cross finial, a square tower with a clock face, pointed-arch openings, and small copper‑green gabled roofs over side portals—features typical of Neo‑Gothic design. Warm low afternoon/evening light highlights the facade, while the street is in shade. On both sides of the street are late‑19th‑century plastered apartment houses with white trim and cornices. Parked cars line the curbs, a zebra crossing is in the foreground, and multiple blue parking signs are visible. A corner street sign reads “Eilert Sundts gate,” confirming the viewpoint down this street toward the church.

photographer_location.name: Eilert Sundts gate 28, Uranienborg/Frogner, Oslo, Norway (looking northeast toward Uranienborg kirke)

details.description: A narrow Oslo street lined with two rows of low, pastel-colored townhouses (left in red/orange, right in pale gray) leads to a prominent red-brick church with a very tall, slender spire topped by a cross. The church tower features a round clock just below the summit, and there are green copper or patinated roof accents on the spire and nearby gables. The facade of the church is bathed in warm, golden light, suggesting late afternoon or sunset. Cars are parked along both sides of the street, and a pedestrian crosswalk is visible in the foreground. The clear blue sky provides a stark backdrop for the vertical tower, making the church the focal point of the composition in an urban Oslo setting.

entities.notes: Brick church with a tall central spire and clock, Neo-Gothic/Gothic Revival influences.

entities.notes: Brick construction with pointed arches and a tall spire typical of Gothic Revival.

details.description: A straight, narrow Oslo street leads to a tall red‑brick Neo‑Gothic church framed symmetrically between 19th‑century stuccoed apartment buildings. Late‑day sunlight warms the church’s façade, highlighting a high base of dark stone, a central entrance flanked by small green‑roofed turrets, a clock on the tower, and a very slender spire topped with a cross. Parked cars line both curbs, a zebra crossing is in the foreground, and Norwegian parking signs are visible. The sky is clear blue, giving the scene a crisp winter/early spring feel.

subject_location.name: Uranienborg Church (Uranienborg kirke), Uranienborg, Frogner, Oslo, Norway

details.description: A narrow Oslo street lined with two rows of white and pale-colored townhouses, converging toward a red-brick church with a tall, slender spire at the far end. The church features a clock face on the tower and a cross atop the spire, with arched doorways and greenish roof accents. In the foreground there is a pedestrian crosswalk and cars parked along both sides of the street, under a clear blue sky.

entities.name: Church (brick, clock-tower)
entities.notes: Brick church with a tall clock-tower spire; visible cross at the top and arched entrances.

details.description: A street-level photograph looking down Holtegata in Oslo towards the Uranienborg Church (Uranienborg kirke). The church stands majestically at the end of the street, its red brick Neo-Gothic façade and tall central spire with a clock face brilliantly illuminated by warm, golden sunlight (likely sunset) against a clear blue sky. The church sits on a hill, elevating it above the street level. The foreground street and flanking buildings are cast in shadow, creating a strong contrast with the sunlit landmark. On the left, a distinctive red residential building with white window frames and decorative molding sits on the corner, bearing a street sign for 'Eilert Sundts gate'. On the right, a classic grey 19th-century apartment building lines the street. A pedestrian crosswalk spans the foreground, and cars are parked along both sides of the road, including a black station wagon driving away in the distance.

details.description: A vertical shot looking down Eilert Sundts gate in Oslo, Norway, towards the Uranienborg Church. The church is a prominent Neo-Gothic red brick structure with a tall, slender spire and a clock on its tower, illuminated by the warm, low-angled light of the setting sun. The street is flanked by multi-story residential buildings in the Historicist style, with the building on the left painted a deep red with white trim and the one on the right in a light grey. Parked cars line both sides of the street, and a pedestrian crosswalk is visible in the foreground. The sky is a clear, pale blue.

details.description: A narrow urban street frames a tall red‑brick Gothic Revival church with a very slender spire and a clock on the tower. Copper-green trim accents the gables and spire base. The church sits on a slightly elevated dark stone base with an arched entrance gate. Low, warm sunlight lights the façade. The surrounding street is lined with late‑19th‑century wooden/stucco townhouses typical of Bergen, creating a symmetrical view toward the church.

details.description: A European street scene dominated by a tall red-brick church tower with a clock face and a slender spire. The tower features Gothic-style elements such as arched openings and pinnacles. Surrounding buildings are pale gray/white with multiple windows and decorative moldings. Cars are parked along the curb, and a pedestrian crosswalk is visible on the street. The sky is clear and blue, with warm sunlight illuminating the brickwork of the church.

details.description: A narrow European city street lined with 3–4 story historic townhouses leads directly to a large red‑brick neo‑Gothic church. The church has a very tall, slender spire with green-copper accents, a clock on the tower, pointed-arch windows, and buttresses. Late-afternoon light illuminates the facade warmly while the street remains in shadow. Parked cars line both curbs, blue parking signs are visible, and a zebra crossing is in the foreground under a clear blue sky.

entities.notes: Tall central spire, pointed-arch windows, buttresses, and clock on the tower.

details.description: A European city street scene featuring a tall orange-brick church tower with a clock and spire at the far end of the street. The tower is flanked by rows of white/gray, ornate multi-story buildings with decorative facades. Cars are parked along one side of the narrow street, and a crosswalk is visible in the foreground. The sky is clear blue, and the scene appears to be captured from a sidewalk corner, with the church tower forming the visual focus in the distance. The image orientation is slightly rotated from a traditional upright view.

details.description: A street-level photograph, rotated 90 degrees clockwise, looking down a shadowed residential street towards a sunlit church tower in the distance. The perspective is framed by tall, 19th-century apartment buildings on either side. On the right (bottom in image orientation), a building with a red facade and white decorative trim features a street sign reading "Eilert Sundts gate". On the left (top in image orientation), a grey/white building with classical detailing lines the street. Parked cars line the curb, including a black station wagon with a visible license plate. At the end of the street, the Uranienborg Church rises prominently, its red brick facade and tall central tower with a green copper spire and clock face glowing in warm, golden-hour sunlight, contrasting with the shaded foreground.

details.description: A vertical eye-level shot looking down Eilert Sundts gate towards the Uranienborg Church in Oslo, Norway. The church is a prominent Neo-Gothic structure built from red brick, featuring a tall, slender spire and a clock tower, illuminated by the warm, low-angled light of the golden hour. The street is flanked by four-to-five-story residential buildings with classical facades, decorative moldings, and stucco finishes in shades of white and terracotta. Parked cars line the right side of the street, and a pedestrian crosswalk is visible in the foreground. The sky is a clear, pale blue.
